ranked_tasks.reasoning: This task carries a critical risk level (risk_001), has rolled over twice indicating persistent blockage, and is blocking 3 downstream dependencies on the NGIS Phase 3 project. The deadline of 2026-03-10 is imminent and the Director has already been briefed, signalling executive visibility and accountability pressure.

An active ITSG-33 IA-5 compliance finding (risk_002) has been flagged by TBS, and the SA&A documentation submission is due 2026-03-12 with zero rollover — meaning this is a new but externally mandated obligation with regulatory consequences if missed. It blocks 2 dependencies and carries the highest stakeholder priority, with the Director already briefed per context.

This task has the earliest deadline in the entire list (2026-03-07) and is directly linked to the critical vendor delay risk (risk_001) affecting Site A migration cutover. Although it blocks no downstream tasks directly, the impact assessment is a prerequisite for informed decision-making on task_001 and the broader NGIS Phase 3 schedule, and its not_started status makes it immediately actionable.

A $340,000 (14%) budget overrun (risk_003) is an active high-level risk requiring Director approval, and the deadline of 2026-03-10 is imminent with one prior rollover. Failure to secure approval blocks 1 downstream dependency and risks project continuity on proj_002, with the budget revision request already documented as of March 3.

The potential departure of the sole AD migration expert (risk_004) represents a critical single point of failure, and with 4 dependencies blocked this task has the highest dependency-blocking count in the list. Although the deadline is 2026-03-17 and rollover count is zero, the not_started status combined with the knowledge transfer lead time required makes this time-sensitive beyond what the deadline alone suggests.

UAT test plan completion is due 2026-03-10 and is currently in progress, blocking 1 downstream dependency. While the risk level is medium and stakeholder priority is lower, the imminent deadline and in-progress status suggest this is on track but requires monitoring to avoid a rollover.

Despite a low risk classification, this task has rolled over 3 times — the highest rollover count in the list — which signals persistent de-prioritisation that may be undermining the resolution of the critical vendor delay (risk_001). No deadline is set and no dependencies are blocked, but the rollover pattern warrants attention and a decision on whether to continue, escalate, or close.

The Treasury Board submission for GC-Cloud Wave 4 has a deadline of 2026-03-15 with no rollover history and no dependencies blocked, placing it in a manageable position. However, TB submissions carry institutional process requirements and lead time, so early initiation is advisable despite the medium risk classification.

End-user communication planning for the data centre migration has a deadline of 2026-04-01, no rollover history, no blocked dependencies, and a low risk level. It is not_started but the deadline provides sufficient runway for initiation after higher-priority tasks are addressed.

Updating project charter formatting and version numbering is an administrative task with no risk level assigned, no dependencies blocked, zero stakeholder priority, and a distant deadline of 2026-04-15. It should be deferred until all substantive project risks and deliverables are addressed.
